Full job description
Head of Operations (Director Designate)

Location: Stone, Staffs

Employment Type: Full-time, permanent

Salary: Up to £125k plus profit share, depending on experience

Holiday: 33 days inclusive of BH

Pension: Contributory NEST Pension

Benefits: Company Sick Pay Scheme, Christmas Shutdown, Annual Salary review

About Us: Tecni-Form is a leading UK manufacturer specializing in rotational moulding and reaction injection moulding (RIM). With over 50 years of experience, we serve diverse sectors including automotive, healthcare, and industrial equipment. Our commitment to quality, innovation, and customer satisfaction has established us as a trusted partner for clients such as JCB, Caterpillar, and Veolia. ​

The Opportunity: The role of Head of Operations (Director Designate) is an opportunity to be part of a committed team, and directly influential in the future policies and direction of company development. It is anticipated that the Head of Operations would in due course be appointed to the board of directors. The post reports to the Managing Director.

You will be working as part of a successful, privately owned, polymer manufacturing company, that genuinely offers and fosters a supportive and forward-thinking work culture. It is somewhere where staff are valued and rewarded for their contribution.

Purpose of the Job

The successful candidate will need to be an aspirational role model to turn enthusiastic and competent managers into great managers and to identify the rising stars for good succession/workforce planning. You will bring a clear and credible management style and culture to Tecni-Form and instil high quality management techniques in the operational teams. You will be supported in this by the current team of committed managers

Key Strategic Manufacturing Responsibilities:

· Directing Business Operations which include, Production, IT, Quality, HSE, HR, & Customer service / planning.

· Direct the full Operations function across multiple lines and shifts.

· Ensure targets (output, quality, efficiency) are consistently achieved or exceeded.

· Maintain and drive Lean Manufacturing, JIT, and continuous improvement methodologies across the plant, including implementation in the present ongoing programme of robotics, advanced manufacturing techniques, and energy resource management.

· Direct planning in collaboration with supply chain, logistics, and engineering teams.

· Ensure compliance with quality standards.

· Support new product introductions and processes and pre-production builds.

· Set high standards of health & safety, working closely with HSE teams to reduce risk and maintain compliance.

· Direct cost reduction initiatives, and productivity improvements.

· Coach, develop, and motivate the production management and teams.

· Direct quality, maintenance, and engineering functions to ensure seamless operations and quick resolution of downtime or quality issues.

· Direct and support cross-functional problem solving, including root cause analysis and corrective actions.

Key Commercial Responsibilities

· Maintain a close relationship with major customers, and be able to represent the company at meetings.

· Collaborate with financial, technical and commercial management in achieving profit performance.

· Ensure delivery on capital expenditure programmes.

· Maintain close working relationship with major suppliers, involving negotiation of on key contracts.

· Work with HR and HS contractors to ensure compliance in safe working conditions and staff relations.

Essential Criteria:

· Extensive experience managing in a manufacturing environment is essential, including at least 5 years in senior management.

· Strong technical and commercial experience

· Results driven with a proven track record of operational management excellence and driving change and culture.

· Comfortable leading and / or mentoring others

· Proactive, curious and solution oriented

· Strong business acumen with a desire to be heavily involved in the Company’s strategy and growth

Person Specification:

· Strategic thinker – you know how to think differently, to reflect and to dig deeper to find the best outcomes when analysing processes, developing metrics, and making informed decisions.

· Strong communicator – you know how to tailor your communications, and you have excellent interpersonal skills. You are tactful, confident, and clear when communicating.

· Confident and inspiring – you are credible, influential, and gain the respect of all your stakeholders. You are confident working autonomously and making recommendations to leadership

· Collaborative – you engender positive working relationships amongst internal teams.

· Educated to Masters, MBA, or equivalent

·

How to Apply:

If you are looking to take the next step in your career, we would love to hear from you. Please use the site application facility or email your CV to hayley@warnerconsulting.co.uk.

Applications will be reviewed as they come in.

2-stage onsite interview process including assessments.

NO AGENCIES PLEASE

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: Up to £125,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Company pension
Profit sharing
Education:

Master's (preferred)
Experience:

senior management in a manufacturing environment: 5 years (preferred)
Work Location: In person
salary: Up to £125,000 a year plus profit share
shortCompanyReason: UK manufacturer with 50+ years' experience in rotational moulding and RIM.
shortRoleReason: Senior operations leader for a manufacturing business, with board progression implied.
companySummary: Tecni-Form is a privately owned UK polymer manufacturer specialising in rotational moulding and reaction injection moulding, serving automotive, healthcare and industrial equipment sectors.
roleSummary: The role leads operations across production and support functions, driving performance, improvement, compliance, and cross-functional coordination, with potential future board appointment.
headlineAttraction: Director Designate route with board potential and a high-impact operations remit.
rolePurpose: To lead and improve the full operations function, develop managers, strengthen culture, and support the company’s growth and strategic direction.
travelRequired: Some meetings with major customers and suppliers implied
advertSummary: Tecni-Form is hiring a Head of Operations (Director Designate) in Stone for a full-time, permanent, on-site role paying up to £125k plus profit share. The role requires senior manufacturing leadership, strong technical and commercial capability, and responsibility for operations, improvement, compliance, customer/supplier relationships, and strategic growth.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this role because it sits right at the point where operational discipline, people leadership and business growth meet. My background is in taking complex operations, tightening control and improving performance without losing sight of the day-to-day realities on the floor.
whyThisRole: The work itself matches what I do best: improving output, quality, cost and control across an operation that depends on strong coordination between production, quality, engineering, planning and commercial teams. I would be comfortable with the mix of hands-on operational leadership, problem solving and people development that this role needs.

Lead the operations function across multiple lines and shifts
Deliver output, quality, and efficiency targets

Lead cross-functional problem solving and corrective actions
Maintain relationships with major customers and suppliers

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operations leader who brings structure, pace and commercial grip to manufacturing growth.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m looking at this role because it sits right at the point where operational discipline, people leadership and business growth meet. My background is in taking complex operations, tightening control and improving performance without losing sight of the day-to-day realities on the floor.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Tecni-Form stands out to me because it is a privately owned UK manufacturer with a long track record, a clear quality focus and a supportive working culture. I also like the fact that the role sits close to the Managing Director and carries genuine future board potential, which tells me the business is serious about succession and long-term leadership.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The work itself matches what I do best: improving output, quality, cost and control across an operation that depends on strong coordination between production, quality, engineering, planning and commercial teams. I would be comfortable with the mix of hands-on operational leadership, problem solving and people development that this role needs.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Senior manufacturing leadership with operational improvement during growth
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations in a specialist manufacturing setting during a scale-up phase, so I understand the pressure of keeping delivery disciplined while the business is growing.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest manufacturing proof point near the top of the page.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led manufacturing operations through growth while keeping delivery and resilience under control.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Commercial awareness, financial control and performance improvement
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: This shows I can connect operational control to financial outcomes, which matters in a role that needs both performance improvement and commercial judgement.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting metric-led example for commercial credibility.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve helped improve profit, cash and working capital by tightening operational control.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Compliance, safety and practical process control
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I built a practical compliance-tracking system in a safety-critical environment, which is relevant to the need for strong HSE and quality discipline.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as evidence of structured control in a regulated or safety-led setting.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve put simple controls in place where compliance and safe progression really mattered.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Leading and mentoring teams through demanding operational cycles
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve led and supported a team through business-critical work while maintaining service standards, which fits the expectation to coach managers and keep the operation steady.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a lighter supporting example for leadership style and workload control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m used to keeping standards up while leading people through demanding periods.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My read is that this role needs someone who can run a manufacturing operation with discipline, improve performance without creating noise, and build stronger managers around them. It also needs someone who can work credibly with technical, commercial and customer stakeholders, while keeping quality, safety and cost under control.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I led day-to-day operations in a specialist manufacturing environment during a scale-up phase, with a focus on delivery discipline, resilience and capacity.
experienceMappings.relevance: This is the closest match to the core manufacturing leadership brief.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I improved operational resilience through changes to processes, equipment, capacity and ways of working while the business was growing.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role asks for continuous improvement and productivity gains, not just steady-state management.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation and released £33.3k of working capital through tighter debtor control.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I understand the link between operational decisions and financial performance.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a tracking system to monitor ride operating hours and support compliance and operator progression in a safety-critical setting.
experienceMappings.relevance: That kind of practical control matters in a plant where quality and HSE standards have to be maintained consistently.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I coordinated workload allocation and training across a 12-person function and supported delivery through annual business-critical projects.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role calls for someone who can develop managers and build capability, not just manage tasks.

focusAreasToBring.title: Operational control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on how work is planned, tracked and escalated so output, quality and efficiency are visible and manageable.

focusAreasToBring.title: Manager development
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would spend time building confidence and accountability in the production management team, because the brief is clear that succession and stronger management matter.

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical improvement
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would look for changes that reduce downtime, improve flow and make the operation easier to run, rather than chasing improvement for its own sake.

focusAreasToBring.title: Commercial discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on the link between operational decisions, cost, service and cash, because that is where performance becomes real.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of manufacturing experience, operational turnaround and financial control that fits the shape of this role well. I’ve worked in environments where performance depends on clear reporting, strong leadership and calm execution, and I’m comfortable being accountable for the full operation rather than one function.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first 6-12 months strengthening day-to-day control, building confidence in the management team and tightening the link between planning, production and support functions. I would also look for practical improvement in quality, cost, downtime and compliance, while getting close to customers and suppliers where that helps the operation run better.
companyHighlights: Serves automotive, healthcare and industrial equipment sectors
Trusted by clients such as JCB, Caterpillar and Veolia
Director Designate route with anticipated board appointment
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work well in environments that value high standards, direct communication and steady improvement. The supportive, forward-thinking culture described here feels like a good fit for the way I lead: clear on expectations, practical in delivery and focused on building capability around me.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the production, quality, HSE, planning and support teams, understand the current constraints, and build a clear view of performance, risks and priorities.
I would focus on reporting, escalation, routines and ownership so the management team has a clearer rhythm for managing output, quality, downtime and issues.
I would agree a small number of practical improvement actions around productivity, compliance, cost or flow, and start building momentum with the team around visible wins.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, commercially aware operations style and a clear focus on making the manufacturing business easier to run, safer to work in and stronger to grow.
closingProofPoints: I’ve led specialist manufacturing operations through scale-up and change.
I’ve helped improve profit, cash and working capital through tighter control.
I’ve built practical compliance and reporting tools in regulated environments.
contentNotes: I kept the company section to facts clearly supported by the advert.
I used the manufacturing example most heavily because it is the strongest direct match; the NHS and leisure examples are supporting evidence for control, compliance and leadership.
No unsupported claims were added about Tecni-Form’s internal structure, strategy or current projects beyond the advert text.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
